jobs.title: Deputy Director, Internal Leadership Communications
jobs.description:
**The Foundation**


We are the largest nonprofit fighting poverty, disease, and inequity around the world. Founded on a simple premise: people everywhere, regardless of identity or circumstances, should have the chance to live healthy, productive lives. We believe our employees should reflect the rich diversity of the global populations we aim to serve. We provide an exceptional benefits package to employees and their families which include comprehensive medical, dental, and vision coverage with no premiums, generous paid time off, paid family leave, foundation\-paid retirement contribution, regional holidays, and opportunities to engage in several employee communities. As a workplace, we’re committed to creating an environment for you to thrive both personally and professionally.

**The Team**


The Communications Division, led by the Chief Communications Officer, oversees the foundation’s global communications strategy. The division brings together internal and external communications to advance the foundation’s mission to create a more equitable world.  

  

The Internal Communications team connects employees to the foundation’s mission, strategy, work, and impact. We partner with leaders and teams across the organization to develop strategic, employee\-centered communications that inform, engage, and inspire. Through thoughtful planning, channel management, leadership communications, storytelling, and enterprise\-wide communications coordination, we help employees understand what matters most, feel connected to our global community, and contribute to a more informed, aligned, and engaged organization.**Application Deadline: Friday, August 14, 2026**

**Your Role**

Reporting to the Director of Internal Communications, you oversee the foundation’s internal leadership communications portfolio—building the systems, strategies, and capabilities that enable leaders across the foundation to communicate with clarity, empathy, and purpose. You are a trusted advisor to senior leaders and a skilled strategist who understands that how leaders communicate is as important as what they communicate.


You develop and manage the frameworks, tools, channels, and content that equip the Executive Leadership Team, and leaders at all levels to engage authentically with employees—during moments of strategic importance, organizational change, and day\-to\-day connection. You bring a sophisticated understanding of leadership communication as a discipline, including how to coach leaders, how to build and sustain credibility through communication, and how to translate complex organizational moments into clear, human narratives.


You are also a resident expert and coach for the team on internal communications platforms, data, and analytics. You help architect and steward the foundation’s internal communications technology ecosystem—including SharePoint and Viva Engage—and you use data and analytics to continuously improve the reach, relevance, and impact of leadership communications. You bring a systems thinker’s mindset to both platform architecture and communications strategy, ensuring the foundation’s channels and content work together to support a coherent and effective employee experience.


As a key member of the Internal Communications leadership team, you model strong judgment, creative problem\-solving, and the ability to lead decisively through ambiguity. This role is designed as a potential succession path for the Director of Internal Communications and offers significant scope, visibility, and opportunity to grow into enterprise\-wide communications leadership.

**What You’ll Do**

jobs.title: Chief Executive Officer
jobs.description:
POSITION SUMMARY
  
  

Laguna Healthcare Corporation, a tribally chartered nonprofit healthcare organization serving the Pueblo of Laguna in Laguna, New Mexico, is seeking a Chief Executive Officer (CEO) to lead the organization through its next phase of growth and help shape its long\-term strategic direction. The organization provides comprehensive primary care, behavioral health, pharmacy, rehabilitation, and preventive health services to the community it serves.
  
  

Position Summary
  
  

The Chief Executive Officer will work closely with Board of Directors to deliver quality healthcare services while executing its long\-term strategic plan. The CEO will assist the Board of Directors in setting the future path of the organization. This position is responsible for the day\-to\-day operations of the Corporation, including oversight of a School Based Health Center, Community Health Clinic, financial management, and programming and community engagement. This position is also tasked with expanding LHC’s services to include a dental clinic and other specialty services such as Obstetrics/Gynecology, Maternal Health, Audiology, Podiatry, Nephrology and Pediatrics.
  
  

**Direct Reports:**  

Chief Operating Officer
  
  

Chief Medical Officer
  
  

Chief Financial Officer
  
  

HR Director
  
  

Executive Assistant
  
  

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
  
  

* Develop and implement the strategic plan and vision set by the Board.
* Prepare activity reports, and work with the Chief Financial Officer (CFO) to prepare financial reports for the monthly meetings of the Board of Directors.
* Assist with interpretation of Federal, State, Tribal and local rules and regulations.
* Determine personnel requirements and ensures that properly qualified individuals occupy each position.
* Ensure the development of HR programs for the successful recruitment, placement and development of qualified personnel.
* Reviews and gives performance evaluations, makes salary recommendations, and resolves personnel problems beyond the responsibility of supervisory staff.
* Prepares required grant applications and budgets to ensure sound financial management and an optimum efficiency of operations.
* Maintains diligent monitoring of all financial activity through the use of a system of responsible accounting, including budget and internal controls, and an annual independent audit.
* Ensures that problems and exceptions noted in the annual audit are thoroughly analyzed and that correcting solutions are implemented.

* Oversees contractors and/or consultants to ensure timely completion of any and all contract activities including budgets and related deliverables.
* Maintains prescribed security controls to protect the Corporation against criminal and fraudulent acts and unnecessary risk or exposure.
* Participates in any required training for all staff and Board members.
* Is present when the Board of Directors provides reports, presentations, and updates to the Governor and/or Council of the Pueblo of Laguna.
* Participates, or is represented, in community, State and national associations and professional activities which define the delivery of health care services and aid in both short and long range planning of health services and facilities.

* Cross\-cultural competency and ability to understand and respond appropriately, effectively, and sensitively to populations served by LHC

jobs.title: Director of Development
jobs.description:
**About Friends of Waterfront Park**

  

Friends of Waterfront Park (Friends), a nonprofit organization, was established in 2012\. City officials and civic leaders recognized that, like all great parks in cities across the country, Waterfront Park needed a nonprofit leader to ensure its lasting success. From our early days of facilitating public engagement around the design of Waterfront Park to our historic fundraising Campaign for Waterfront Park and our development of public programs and public safety strategies centered in compassion, Friends has been a key partner to the City of Seattle and communities across the city in an unprecedented effort to build an extraordinary urban public space.

  

In 2025, the Waterfront Park Grand Opening Celebration brought more than 50,000 people to celebrate a new era for Seattle’s central waterfront. With the $170M Campaign complete and Waterfront Park open to the community, Friends is focused on welcoming everyone to this iconic Seattle gem all year through fundraising, programming, public safety investments, park experience, and long\-term stewardship.

 **Our Mission** affirms why we’re here: to bring people and local communities together to ensure Waterfront Park thrives as a vibrant gathering space that fosters connections, and honor Seattle’s natural beauty and stories.

 **Our Vision** imagines where we’re headed. Seattle is transformed by Waterfront Park, a public space sparking joy, connection, and belonging with every visit.

 **Our Values** of Community, Equity, Care, Collaboration, and Adaptability ground how we work, from programming and partnerships to public safety and storytelling.

  

Friends of Waterfront Park recognizes and acknowledges historic and existing systemic racism embedded in our city. Friends commits to being an anti\-racist organization by prioritizing racial equity within the organization and in the public spaces we operate.

 **Land Acknowledgement**

  

Waterfront Park is situated on the land of the Coast Salish Peoples, who have resided here since time immemorial and continue to thrive. With respect and humility, we acknowledge the history of the waterfront, the dispossession of land from the Coast Salish people and, most importantly, the strength and resilience of Native people and their culture through this history and to the present.

 **Position Summary**

  

Friends of Waterfront Park is seeking a strategic, relationship\-driven **Director of Development** to lead and grow its philanthropy revenue program. Reporting to the CEO, the Director of Development will oversee and grow a **$3\.66M annual fundraising portfolio** and lead a philanthropy team spanning major gifts, leadership giving, events, sponsorships, grants, annual giving, and database functions. Serving as a member of the Senior Leadership Team, the Director contributes to organizational strategy, alignment, and culture while advancing Friends’ philanthropic vision. This is a role for an experienced fundraiser who brings both the relationship skills to personally manage major donor portfolios and to develop and execute ongoing donor cultivation and stewardship strategies. They also bring the management breadth to provide strategic oversight of a full function development team. They are energized by the challenge of building systems, developing people, and stewarding major donor relationships simultaneously.

 **This is a full\-time, exempt, hybrid role based in Seattle, WA.**

 **The Opportunity**

  

Friends of Waterfront Park is a growing organization with a total operating budget of $9\.3M, now entering an exciting new chapter following the completion of its historic $170M capital campaign.



The Director of Development will lead an experienced philanthropy team through a significant transition: modernizing development operations and data systems, building out a comprehensive annual giving and grants program, refining the events strategy, and stewarding the major donor relationships that are foundational to Friends' long\-term success.

  

The Director will have meaningful visibility with the Board of Directors and the Development Committee, working in close partnership with the CEO to align fundraising strategy with organizational priorities. Friends' signature fundraising events, the Pier Party and Dinner with Friends, anchor the summer calendar and represent significant donor engagement opportunities the Director will help shape.

 **The Fundraising Portfolio**

  

The Director of Development will lead strategy and relationship management across Friends’ fundraising program. While the 2027 portfolio may adjust in scope, the 2026 portfolio totals approximately $3\.66M in annual fundraising and includes:

  

* Leadership Circle: $1,200,000 (33%)
* Fundraising Events (Key events include Pier Party, Dinner with Friends): $990,000 (27%)
* Major Gifts: $700,000 (19%)
* New Grants / Institutional Funding: $600,000 (16%)
* Annual Giving and Board Giving: $172,500 (5%)

  



The Director will personally manage a portfolio of approximately 100 top donors and prospects. The top 25 relationships will be co\-managed in close coordination with the CEO and Board; the remaining 75 donors and prospects will sit in the Director's independent portfolio. The Director will also staff and support the Development Committee.

  

In addition, the Director will provide stewardship oversight for previous campaign contributions ($2,000,000\) and secured grants ($1,853,238\) currently in the budget. Candidates should be prepared to engage with donors and funders across the full scope of Friends’ philanthropy revenue.

  

**Responsibilities**

 **Fundraising Strategy and Leadership**

  

* Lead and grow Friends’ philanthropy revenue program across major gifts, leadership giving, annual fund, institutional funding, and events.
* Develop a multiyear fundraising strategy that transitions Friends from capital campaign mode to sustained operational philanthropy.
*

jobs.description:
**Chief Philanthropy Officer**

**About The Virginia Home**

The mission of The Virginia Home is rooted in the belief that every individual deserves a life filled with purpose, connection, and respect. Founded more than a century ago through a bold vision to provide compassionate, lifelong care for individuals with disabilities, The Virginia Home has long been a place of dignity, innovation, and community. As we embark on our most ambitious capital campaign to date to build a new, innovative campus, we are creating more than a space, we are shaping a future where residents can experience greater independence, enriched programming, and a vibrant community designed around their needs. This moment marks a transformative step forward in honoring our residents and expanding our impact for generations to come.

**Position Summary**

The Chief Philanthropy Officer (CPO) serves as the senior fundraising leader for The Virginia Home and is responsible for the strategy, execution, and performance of all philanthropic revenue in support of the organization’s mission and capital campaign. This role provides overall leadership for the advancement function, including major and principal gifts, annual giving, campaign strategy, and donor engagement.

Working in close partnership with the CEO, Board leadership, and campaign consultants, the CPO will lead the execution of a transformational capital campaign while building a high\-performing, metrics\-driven fundraising team and culture.

**Duties/Responsibilities:**

**Campaign Leadership \& Strategy**

* Lead execution of the organization’s capital campaign strategy in partnership with the CEO and Board leadership.
* Finalize and refine campaign goals, timeline, and key milestones.
* Ensure alignment between campaign priorities, annual fundraising goals, and donor strategy.
* Drive overall campaign momentum, pacing, and accountability.

**Principal Gift Leadership**

* Maintain a personal portfolio of the organization’s high\-capacity donors, including individuals, foundations, and corporations.
* Help develop strategy for Chief Executive Officer’s engagement with top\-level prospects and donors.
* Lead strategy and solicitation for principal gifts ($500K\+), including joint donor engagement with CEO, Board members and senior volunteers.
* Serve as the organization’s lead fundraiser for transformational gifts for both the annual fund and campaign utilizing all giving vehicles, including planned giving.

**Team Leadership \& Accountability**

* Provide direct supervision and leadership to philanthropy staff.
* Establish and reinforce clear portfolio management expectations, performance metrics, and accountability systems.
* Lead weekly team meetings focused on pipeline progress, strategy, and donor movement.
* Facilitate monthly one\-on\-one meetings with all direct reports to review metric performance and strategy.
* Foster a results\-oriented, collaborative, and donor\-centered team culture.

**Board \& Volunteer Engagement**

* Partner with the Campaign Cabinet and Board leadership to activate volunteer fundraising efforts.
* Prepare and support Board members and senior volunteers for cultivation and solicitation opportunities.
* Serve as a key liaison between staff and volunteer leadership to advance campaign goals.

**Revenue Oversight \& Reporting**

* Oversee all fundraising revenue and ensure alignment with organizational and campaign goals.
* Track campaign progress and ensure clear, consistent reporting on pipeline, solicitations, and closed gifts.
* Provide monthly fundraising updates to executive leadership and the Board.
* Utilize data to inform strategy, prioritize opportunities, and forecast results.

**Preferred Attributes**

* Proven ability to lead high\-performing fundraising teams.
* Strong presence and ability to engage effectively with Board members and senior stakeholders.
* Highly strategic with strong execution discipline.
* Collaborative, results\-oriented, and mission\-driven.

**Education and Experience:**

* Bachelor’s degree required.
* 10\+ years of progressive fundraising experience, including senior leadership roles.
* Demonstrated success leading major and principal gift fundraising efforts, including securing seven\-figure gifts.
* Experience leading or participating in a capital campaign.
* Strong leadership, strategic thinking, and relationship management skills.

jobs.title: Executive Director
jobs.description:
**MEDICAL RESCUE TEAM SOUTH AUTHORITY**

**EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR**

**PITTSBURGH, PA**




**ORGANIZATION:**

Medical Rescue Team South Authority (MRTSA), founded in 1976, provides health care to patients in the prehospital setting. As a part of the health care delivery system, MRTSA’s services help maintain a healthy and productive society. With a great emphasis on the compassion and skill of its trained staff, MRTSA is continually seeking ways to share its professionalism with the community at large. MRTSA is committed to the continuous improvement of preventive and dynamic health care services in ways that will contribute to a seamless transition of patients through the continuum of care. To learn more, please visit: http://www.mrtsa.com/home.


**Position Summary**


Medical Rescue Team South Authority seeks an accomplished, mission\-driven Executive Director to serve as the organization’s chief executive and senior administrative leader. Reporting directly to the Board of Directors, the Executive Director is responsible for the strategic, financial, and administrative leadership of MRTSA and for ensuring that the organization has the people, resources, systems, partnerships, and governance support needed to deliver reliable, high\-quality emergency medical services to the communities it serves.


The Executive Director oversees organizational planning, finance and budgeting, human resources and labor relations, Board support, vendor and contract administration, process improvement, and community and stakeholder relations. The Executive Director works in close partnership with other MRTSA leaders to align operational performance, workforce strategy, and fiscal stewardship with MRTSA’s mission and long\-term sustainability.




**Core Responsibilities**

* Serve as the chief executive officer of MRTSA and principal administrative partner to the Board of Directors.
* Lead strategic planning and annual organizational goal setting; translate Board direction into executable plans, priorities, and performance expectations.
* Prepare and present annual operating and capital budgets, multi\-year forecasts, and regular financial reports to the Board; monitor revenues, expenses, cash position, capital needs, and budget\-to\-actual performance.
* Oversee financial administration, including internal controls, audit support, procurement, contract administration, vendor oversight, and coordination with billing, legal, accounting, technology, and other third\-party partners.
* Partner with operational leadership to establish and monitor service, workforce, safety, quality, and financial performance metrics; use data to identify risks, improve processes, and strengthen organizational performance.
* Lead human resources administration for the organization, including staff supervision, performance management, investigations, policy administration, and labor relations, and support collective bargaining, grievance processes, and discipline consistent with law, policy, and the applicable CBA.
* Build and maintain strong working relationships with the Board, partner municipalities, hospitals, county and regional EMS/public\-safety partners, regulators, vendors, and community stakeholders.
* Support sustainable revenue and organizational resilience through reimbursement oversight, grant development and administration, membership/subscription growth, municipal and community partnership development, and other mission\-aligned funding opportunities.
* Ensure MRTSA policies, procedures, and administrative practices are current, well\-documented, compliant, and operationally effective.
* Represent MRTSA professionally at meetings, public events, and interagency forums.




**Required Qualifications**

* Bachelor’s degree in business administration, public administration, healthcare administration, finance, management, or a related field. Significant EMS Leadership Experience may be substituted for degree qualifications by the search committee.
* At least five to ten years of progressive leadership experience in EMS, healthcare, public safety, municipal government, nonprofit management, or another complex 24/7 service environment.
* Demonstrated success in budget development, financial reporting, forecasting, and executive\-level decision support.
* Experience leading staff, supervising managers, and navigating employee relations in a unionized or similarly regulated environment.
* Experience working directly with a governing board, committee structure, or elected/public\-sector stakeholders.
* Experience managing vendors, contracts, and cross\-functional organizational operations.
* Excellent written, verbal, and interpersonal communication skills, including the ability to present clearly to the Board and external stakeholders.
* Valid Pennsylvania driver’s license, or the ability to obtain one within a defined period after hire.




**Preferred Qualifications**

* Master’s degree such as an MBA, MPA, or MHA.
* Familiarity with Pennsylvania EMS, ambulance reimbursement and billing, municipal authorities, public\-safety partnerships, and grant administration.
* Experience leading organizational change, process improvement, workforce stabilization, and culture\-building initiatives.
* Prior experience in a mission\-driven healthcare or public\-service organization with multiple revenue sources and capital\-planning needs.




**Compensation:**

* $125,000\.00 to $140,000\.00 dependent upon qualifications and experience

**To Apply:**

Please send cover letter and resume to:


Search Committee


Medical Rescue Team South Authority


315 Cypress Way


Pittsburgh, Pa. 15228

jobs.title: Executive Director of Philanthropy
jobs.description:
The Director of Philanthropy provides strategic and operational leadership for the Heart of the Valley YMCA’s Annual Campaign, individual annual giving, corporate philanthropic support, event sponsorships, donor stewardship, volunteer fundraising, and related revenue\-generating initiatives.


Reporting to the Chief Mission Advancement Officer, the Director develops and executes the organization’s Annual Giving Campaign and additional year\-round fundraising strategies designed to increase donor acquisition, retention, engagement, and giving. The position manages a portfolio of individual and corporate donors and prospects, cultivates new philanthropic relationships, and stewards existing supporters by meaningfully connecting them to the YMCA’s mission and community impact.


The Director leads the Association\-wide Annual Campaign and works closely with branch leaders, staff, board members, and campaign volunteers to strengthen a culture of philanthropy throughout the organization.


Corporate partnership responsibilities are specifically centered on generating charitable revenue through Annual Campaign gifts, event sponsorships, and other approved mission\-aligned giving opportunities. This includes identifying and soliciting new corporate partners, renewing and growing existing partnerships, coordinating sponsorship fulfillment, and stewarding corporate donor relationships.


The Director serves as a visible ambassador for the Heart of the Valley YMCA and helps connect individuals, businesses, volunteers, and community leaders with opportunities to advance youth development, healthy living, and social responsibility throughout the community.

**OUR CULTURE:**  

Our mission and core values are brought to life by our culture. In the Y, we strive to live our cause of strengthening communities with purpose and intentionality every day. **We are welcoming:** we are open to all. We are a place where you can belong and become. **We are genuine:** we value you and embrace your individuality. **We are hopeful:** we believe in you and your potential to become a catalyst in the world. **We are nurturing:** we support you in your journey to develop your full potential. **We are determined:** above all else, we are on a relentless quest to make our community stronger, beginning with you.

**ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS:**

**Annual Campaign Leadership**

* Lead the planning, implementation, management, and evaluation of the Association\-wide Annual Campaign.
* Develop annual campaign strategies, goals, budgets, timelines, work plans, and performance benchmarks in partnership with the Chief Mission Advancement Officer.
* Manage the Annual Campaign as a year\-round program of donor identification, cultivation, solicitation, stewardship, renewal, and upgrading.
* Develop strategies to increase charitable revenue, donor acquisition and retention, leadership (ESLT) annual giving, average gift size, and staff, board, and volunteer engagement.
* Coordinate multi\-channel fundraising appeals and maintain an annual campaign calendar integrating communications, sponsorship solicitation, stewardship, and volunteer activities.
* Monitor campaign performance and recommend strategic adjustments aligned with organizational priorities and community impact goals.

**Individual Giving and Donor Portfolio Management**

* Manage a portfolio of Annual Campaign, leadership annual giving, and prospective donors through qualification, cultivation, solicitation, stewardship, renewal, and upgrading.
* Maintain meaningful year\-round donor relationships through personal visits, calls, written communications, YMCA experiences, and impact reporting.
* Identify prospective major\-gift donors and coordinate continued cultivation with the Chief Mission Advancement Officer.
* Conduct individual and collaborative gift solicitations with organizational leadership, volunteers, and board members.
* Maintain accurate donor records and activity within the YMCA's donor management system while ensuring confidentiality and professional stewardship.

**Corporate Philanthropy and Event Sponsorships**

* Develop, cultivate, solicit, and steward corporate donors, sponsors, and business partners in support of Annual Campaign giving, event sponsorships, employee\-giving initiatives, matching gifts, and other approved charitable investments.

jobs.title: Director of Operations
jobs.description:
**Director of Operations** 

**Full\-Time \| On\-Site \| Asheville, NC** 

**Help Drive Our Mission Forward** 

At Working Wheels, we believe reliable transportation changes lives. Through our Vehicle Purchase Program and Vehicle Repair Program, we help hardworking individuals and families gain access to safe, dependable vehicles so they can get to work, school, medical appointments, and care for their families.

We're seeking an experienced, mission\-driven Director of Operations to help lead our organization into its next chapter. This is an opportunity for someone who enjoys leading people, improving systems, making decisions, and finding practical solutions in a fast\-paced environment.

**About the Role** 

The Director of Operations provides day\-to\-day leadership for Working Wheels' operations, overseeing the office, automotive dealership, in\-house repair shop, and community programs. This role is responsible for the successful operation of the Vehicle Purchase Program, Vehicle Repair Program, and the sale of refurbished vehicles to the public.

The Director of Operations leads and supports a multidisciplinary team, fostering a collaborative, positive workplace culture. Direct reports include the Director of Vehicle Operations, who manages the daily operations of the repair shop; the Vehicle Repair Program Coordinator, who oversees the Vehicle Repair Program; the Vehicle Donation Coordinator, who manages the vehicle donation process; the Administrative Specialist, who provides administrative and operational support; and the HR/Finance Coordinator, who manages human resources and financial administration.

In addition to personnel leadership, the Director of Operations oversees the organization's financial systems, human resources functions, and operational infrastructure. As the primary administrator of the organization's technology platforms and data systems, this role ensures accurate reporting, supports grant compliance and program evaluation, and uses data to inform operational and strategic decision\-making. The Director of Operations also cultivates relationships with community partners to strengthen collaboration and expand the organization's impact.

**We're Looking for Someone Who...** 

● Is passionate about serving the community through nonprofit work.

● Leads with integrity, empathy, and accountability.

● Enjoys leading and developing people.

● Takes initiative and makes thoughtful, strategic decisions

● Looks for opportunities to improve processes and build effective systems.

● Communicates well and builds strong relationships with staff, program participants, volunteers, donors, and community partners.

● Can manage multiple priorities while staying organized.

● Brings a positive, solutions\-focused attitude to every challenge.

**Qualifications**

*Required*

● Bachelor's degree or an equivalent combination of education and experience.

● Three or more years of operations management experience.

● Experience supervising and developing employees.

● Experience with budgeting, financial oversight, and organizational planning.

● Experience with SaaS (Software as a Service) for project management (e.g., Asana, monday.com)

● Strong organizational, communication, and problem\-solving skills.

● Experience with Google Workspace.

● Valid driver's license.

*Preferred* 

● Experience with and interest in automobiles.

● Nonprofit leadership experience.

● Experience with grant administration or reporting.

**Compensation \& Benefits** 

We value our employees and offer a comprehensive compensation package that includes:

● Salary $71,000 \- $75,000

● Monthly taxable healthcare support stipend ($540/month)

● 16 days of paid time off (PTO) during the first year of employment, increasing with tenure

● Paid Holidays including your birthday

● Retirement plan with a 3% employer contribution

● Short\-term disability coverage

● Access to voluntary dental, vision, critical illness, and accident insurance plans

● Opportunities for professional development and continued growth

**Why Asheville?** 

Nestled in the Blue Ridge Mountains, Asheville offers an incredible quality of life with endless opportunities for outdoor recreation, live music, local restaurants, and a vibrant arts community. It's a place where you can build a meaningful career while enjoying everything Western North Carolina has to offer.

**Join Our Team** 

Working Wheels is more than a nonprofit; we're a team committed to helping people move forward. If you're a collaborative leader who enjoys building strong teams, improving organizations, and making a lasting impact in the community, we'd love to hear from you.

**How to Apply**

Email one document containing your cover letter and resume to gillian@workingwheelswnc.org

Subject line: Director of Operations – \[Your Last Name]

In your cover letter, please share:

● How did you hear about the role

● How does your experience align with the position

● Why are you interested in Working Wheels

*Applications without a cover letter will not be considered. No phone inquiries, please.*

Working Wheels is an equal opportunity employer and encourages applicants from diverse backgrounds.

Pay: $71,000\.00 \- $75,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* 401(k) matching
* Paid time off
* Professional development assistance
* Retirement plan

People with a criminal record are encouraged to apply

Experience:

* Supervising: 3 years (Preferred)
* SaaS: 1 year (Preferred)
* Program management: 3 years (Preferred)
* Google Workspace: 1 year (Preferred)
* Nonprofit management: 1 year (Preferred)

Work Location: In person

jobs.title: Program Director, Ed.D. - Artificial Intelligence & Moral Agency (ShawU Divinity School)
jobs.description:
Job Summary:



Reporting to the Dean of the Divinity School, The Program Director provides academic leadership and
  

day\-to\-day administrative oversight for the EdD in Artificial Intelligence and Moral Agency, SUDS's
  

first doctoral\-level offering. This person sets the academic vision for the program in partnership with the
  

Dean, ensures the curriculum (theological ethics foundations in Year 1, AI applications and human
  

agency in Year 2, research methods and colloquy in Year 3, and dissertation research in Year 4\) is
  

delivered with rigor, and personally teaches, advises, and chairs dissertations within a cohort model
  

designed for bi\-vocational adult learners.  

Because this is a launch\-phase program, the Director will also help build the infrastructure, faculty
  

bench, and external partnerships (e.g., with AI\-ethics centers and technical institutions) that the degree
  

will depend on for the next several admissions cycles.



Essential Job Functions:  

* Provide overall academic leadership for the EdD program, including curriculum oversight,

 learning outcomes, and program review in coordination with the Dean and institutional
  

 accreditation requirements.  

* Recruit, mentor, and evaluate full\-time and adjunct faculty teaching in the program; build a

 roster of instructors with combined strength in theological ethics and AI literacy.  

* Teach assigned doctoral seminars and chair or serve on dissertation committees.
* Oversee admissions review for the program in partnership with the Admissions Office, applying

 the program's criteria (graduate theological degree, five years of bi\-vocational leadership
  

 experience, GPA/equivalency standards).  

* Coordinate the three\-year residency sequence and annual cohort colloquy, including scheduling,

 faculty assignments, and student progress tracking toward the 45\-credit (36 coursework plus 9
  

 dissertation) requirement.  

* Serve as the primary point of contact for prospective students, partner institutions, and

 accreditation bodies regarding the EdD program.  

* Develop and manage the program budget, including faculty contracts, library/AI\-tool licensing,

 and conference or professional\-development support for faculty and students.  

* Build and maintain relationships with AI\-ethics research centers, technology partners, and

 continuing\-education providers (e.g., for faculty AI\-ethics certification) to keep curriculum
  

 current.  

* Represent the program at conferences, denominational meetings, and academic associations

 relevant to theological education and AI ethics.  

* Other duties as assigned.


Supervisory Responsibilities
  

FT Faculty, PT\-Adjunct Faculty



Minimum Job Qualifications
  

**Required**  

* Earned doctorate (PhD, ThD, EdD, or DMin) in theology, religious studies, Christian ethics, or a

 closely related field.  

* A minimum of five years of progressively responsible experience in theological or graduate

 higher education, including teaching at the graduate level.  

* Demonstrated experience advising, mentoring, or chairing graduate\-level research or

 dissertations.  

* Working knowledge of core AI ethics concepts and frameworks (e.g., algorithmic fairness, bias

 and discrimination, accountability and transparency standards such as the NIST AI Risk
  

 Management Framework) sufficient to guide curriculum and faculty hiring, or a documented
  

 plan to acquire this through certificate study.  

* Demonstrated understanding of, and respect for, bi\-vocational and pastoral ministry contexts and

 the needs of adult professional learners.  

* Strong organizational, budgetary, and personnel\-management skills appropriate to launching and

 sustaining a new doctoral program.

**Preferred**  

* Professional experience outside a formal religious setting (e.g., healthcare, education, criminal

 justice, technology, public policy, or nonprofit leadership) that informs an applied,
  

 interdisciplinary approach to AI ethics.  

* Record of scholarly publication or conference presentation in theological ethics, AI ethics, or a

 related interdisciplinary field (e.g., in venues such as Journal of Religion and Health, AI and
  

 Ethics, Journal of Moral Theology, or at conferences such as AIES or FAccT).  

* Completed certificate, fellowship, or coursework in AI ethics or related technology policy from a

 recognized institution.  

* Ordination, commissioning, or comparable standing within a faith community.
* Experience with online or hybrid doctoral program delivery and learning\-management systems.
* Experience building external partnerships with research centers, denominational bodies, or

 industry organizations.
**Other Competencies (skills, abilities, behavior)**  

Must have a philosophy that is consistent with the Mission, Vision and Core Values of the
  

organization. Vision, commitment, and experience in achieving goals and pursuing excellence is
  

critical: ability to work both independently and collaboratively in a dynamic and demanding
  

environment. Must have strong verbal and written communication and organizational skills; proven
  

ability to manage multiple complex assignments with sensitivity to deadlines, priorities, and
  

changing circumstances; demonstrated professional work ethic and positive, problem\-solving
  

attitude. Must be able to read, write and speak fluently in English.


**Physical Demands**  

Ability to sit or stand for up to 8 hours/day. Some lifting (up to 25 pounds occasionally), bending, and
  

carrying. The employee is regularly required to talk and hear, and to use hands and fingers to handle or
  

feel.

#### **Experience**

* Demonstrated experience advising, mentoring, or chairing graduate\-level research or dissertations.

jobs.description:
**Associate Director of Development Operations**


At Communities In Schools (CIS) of Chicago, we believe students can enhance their learning when their needs are met, and that includes mental and emotional health. Every day, we partner with Chicago Public Schools (CPS) and hundreds of community organizations to bring no\-cost, high\-quality programs directly to schools. These programs help students stay focused, engaged, and on track to graduate.


During the 2025–26 school year, we served over 80,000 students across 250 schools, connecting them to life\-changing support from arts and academic enrichment to health and mental wellness services. And we need your help to make it happen.


The mission of Communities In Schools of Chicago is to surround students with a community of support, empowering them to stay in school and achieve in life. The vision of CIS of Chicago is to ensure that every public school student in Chicago graduates from high school prepared to succeed. The organization's core values are Impact, Integrity, Relationships, and Social Justice.


**Position Overview**


We're seeking an **Associate Director of Development Operations** to join our high\-performing Development team. This role is ideal for a strategic, detail\-oriented operations professional who enjoys improving systems, leveraging data to drive fundraising success, and bringing large\-scale donor events to life. As the operational backbone of the Development team, you'll oversee donor database management, fundraising operations, gift processing, reporting and analytics, and event logistics while ensuring the team has the tools, processes, and insights needed to cultivate and steward donors effectively.


**Your Core Responsibilities**


Development Operations \& CRM:

* Develop reports, dashboards, and donor analyses to inform fundraising strategy.

* Train Development team members on CRM best practices and data management.

Fundraising Operations:

* Manage pledge tracking, reporting, and fundraising analytics.
* Continuously improve operational processes and team workflows.

Financial \& Cross\-Functional Support:

* Serve as the primary liaison with Finance for reconciliations, budgeting, gift reporting, and financial tracking.
* Coordinate data requests and support special projects across departments.

Event Engagement:


* Lead planning and execution of donor cultivation events, including vendor management, logistics, registration, and post\-event reporting.
* Support volunteer committees and attend select evening or weekend events as needed.

**What You Bring:**

* Bachelor's degree
* 5\-7 years of experience in development operations, fundraising, database management, event management, or a related field.
* Experience with donor CRM systems (Salesforce preferred).
* Strong project management, analytical, and organizational skills.
* Advanced Microsoft Office skills, particularly Excel.
* Exceptional attention to detail and the ability to manage multiple priorities.
* Commitment to the mission of Communities In Schools of Chicago.

 **What You'll Get:**

* Salary: $70,000
* Comprehensive benefits, including health insurance, retirement plan, and generous paid time off.
* A collaborative community of educators, nonprofit leaders, and partners working together to create lasting change across Chicago.

*Communities In Schools of Chicago is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace where all individuals feel valued, respected, and supported. We prohibit discrimination and harassment of any kind based on sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, pregnancy (including childbirth and related medical conditions), race, color, ethnicity, national origin, religion, age, disability, medical condition, marital or family status, veteran or military status, citizenship, genetic information, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law.*

*This commitment applies to every aspect of employment at CIS of Chicago\-including recruitment, hiring, training, promotion, compensation, and all other conditions of employment. We believe that building a workforce that reflects the communities we serve strengthens our mission to surround students with a community of support, empowering them to stay in school and achieve in life.*

The Director serves as a key external ambassador for the organization, cultivating high\-impact relationships with donors, funders, corporate leaders, and community stakeholders while partnering closely with the Executive Director and Board to achieve ambitious fundraising goals.**Job Description**


The Director of Development is responsible for leading and executing City Year Rhode Island's comprehensive fundraising strategy to advance the organization's mission and long\-term financial sustainability. Reporting to the Executive Director and serving as a member of the Senior Leadership Team, the Director leads a diversified fundraising program that includes individual giving, corporate partnerships, foundation and government grants, donor stewardship, fundraising events, and Advisory Board engagement.


Working in close partnership with the Executive Director, Advisory Board, and City Year's national development team, the Director cultivates strategic relationships that generate philanthropic investment and deepen community engagement. The Director develops and implements data\-informed fundraising strategies, manages a robust donor pipeline, and strengthens the systems, partnerships, and fundraising capacity needed to achieve annual revenue goals and advance City Year Rhode Island's strategic priorities.


As a key external representative of the organization, the Director builds and maintains strong relationships with donors, funders, corporate and community partners, and civic leaders. This role is responsible for fostering a culture of philanthropy across the organization and ensuring fundraising efforts align with City Year's values, strategic plan, and commitment to service and educational equity.

**Benefits**


Full\-time employees will be eligible for all benefits including vacation, sick days and organization holidays. For more information, click here.


Employment at City Year is at\-will.


City Year does not sponsor work authorization visas.

jobs.title: Executive Director, Workforce Engagement
jobs.description:
**Job no:** 495154  

**Work type:** Administrative (Full Time)  

**Location:** Indianapolis, IN, Hybrid (3\-4 Office / 1\-2 Remote), Other  

**Categories:** Student Development/Residence Life  


**Job Title:** Executive Director, Workforce Engagement


**Reporting Relationship:** Vice President of Learner Success \& Retention


**Unit:** National \& Global


**Department:** Learner Success \- IWU National \& Global


**Campus Location:** Indianapolis Ed Center North, Indianapolis, IN



**Summary of Position:** The Executive Director of Workforce Engagement provides strategic leadership for the Department of Career Readiness and Workforce Launch within IWU National and Global. Reporting directly to the Vice President of Learner Success and Retention, this role is responsible for the design, implementation, and continuous improvement of a three\-pillar career launch system that transforms career preparation from a passive, transactional service into a lifecycle\-integrated, data\-driven intervention model. The Executive Director ensures that every IWU student graduates with a verified Human Capital Package and an active advocate working on their behalf in the regional labor market.



This position serves as the senior strategic leader for the department, overseeing a lean team of 1\.0 to 6\.0 FTE across three operational pillars: Career Clarity, Professional Development, and Market Advocacy. The Executive Director is the primary institutional liaison for the circular talent ecosystem, connecting employer intelligence from Regional Career Agents back to academic curriculum and enrollment strategy. The role operates at the intersection of student success, workforce development, and institutional advancement, requiring the ability to engage credibly at the C\-suite level with employer partners while maintaining deep alignment with IWU's mission of student flourishing.



This is a foundational leadership role in a newly approved, purpose\-built department. The individual selected will have the opportunity to design the model, build the team, and establish the operational infrastructure — not simply execute a pre\-existing system.  

Christian Commitment Requirement: In accordance with Indiana Wesleyan University's employment policy, all employees must possess a strong Christian commitment and adhere to the standards outlined in the IWU Community Lifestyle Statement.


**Duties and Responsibilities**

* Strategic Leadership and Department Vision
	+ Lead the implementation and ongoing refinement of the Career Readiness and Workforce Launch model, ensuring alignment with IWU National and Global's enrollment, retention, and student success goals
	+ Develop and execute a 24\-month implementation roadmap, managing phase transitions from foundation to full\-scale operation with clear milestone accountability
	+ Establish and maintain the department's key performance indicator framework, including placement rates, employer satisfaction scores, Human Capital Package completion rates, and 90\-day graduate retention metrics
	+ Produce a termly Student Success Intelligence Brief that synthesizes workforce outcomes data for VP\-level and Board\-level audiences, translating complex placement and retention data into actionable institutional intelligence
	+ Represent the department in cross\-divisional governance structures, including the Digital Credential Wallet steering committee and the Partnership\-to\-Placement integration with the Enrollment Division
	+ Manage the department budget within approved parameters, applying data\-validated expansion triggers to authorize FTE growth from 4\.0 to 7\.0 as enrollment and caseload thresholds are reached
* Employer Partnership Development and Market Advocacy
	+ Cultivate and manage high\-value employer partnerships at the C\-suite and senior HR level, positioning IWU graduates as verified talent assets through the Human Capital Package framework
	+ Oversee the Regional Career Agent team's employer account portfolio, ensuring consistent relationship management across 15 to 30 active accounts per FTE
	+ Coordinate weekly with the Enrollment Division's Partnership Executive team to align on shared employer account management, relationship ownership protocols, and market intelligence sharing through Salesforce
	+ Provide monthly market intelligence briefs to the Vice President of Learner Success and Retention, summarizing employer workforce needs, hiring trends, and graduate performance data
	+ Lead the institution's participation in regional employer engagement events, workforce development convenings, and industry partnership initiatives across the Indianapolis, Chicago, and Cleveland hub markets
* Team Leadership and Pillar Oversight
	+ Provide direct supervision, coaching, and professional development for all three pillar roles — Career Clarity Specialists, Professional Development Specialists, and Regional Career Agents
	+ Establish and maintain standardized intervention protocols, caseload management practices, and documentation standards across all three pillars
	+ Monitor pillar\-level KPIs on a monthly basis, identifying performance gaps and implementing corrective action plans as needed
	+ Facilitate weekly team coordination meetings and bi\-weekly one\-on\-one supervision sessions with each direct report
	+ Lead the onboarding and training of all new department hires, ensuring alignment with the Human\-First AI philosophy and the Intervention Science framework that underpins the department's operating model
* Technology Infrastructure and AI Augmentation
	+ Oversee the department's AI\-augmented technology stack, including the Student Insights Agent, EdSights early alert integration, and Salesforce CRM workflows, ensuring tools are deployed to maximize human capacity for high\-stakes student interventions
	+ Lead the department's participation in the Digital Credential Wallet initiative, coordinating with Talent Studio, Academics, and Academic Record

new_jobs.description:
**To Apply:** Submit your Resume, Cover Letter and 3 Professional References electronically to **CEOJOB@sgvpartnership.org** by **July 19, 2026 for priority review**. We will continue to accept applications after this date, however they will be reviewed after the *priority* submissions.

This position will remain open until filled.

***\-\-Applications will ONLY be accepted via email, applications submitted through Indeed will NOT be reviewed\-\-***

**About the Opportunity**

The San Gabriel Valley Economic Partnership (SGVEP) is seeking an experienced, visionary, and collaborative leader to serve as its next President \& Chief Executive Officer. For more than 30 years, the Partnership has served as the leading voice for business and economic development in the San Gabriel Valley, representing 31 cities and nearly two million residents throughout eastern Los Angeles County.

**Position Overview**

Reporting directly to the Board of Directors, the President \& CEO serves as the chief executive officer of the organization and is responsible for providing strategic leadership, organizational management, fiscal oversight, membership growth, stakeholder engagement, advocacy, and program development.

The successful candidate will be a dynamic relationship\-builder who can effectively work with elected officials, business leaders, educational institutions, workforce partners, and community stakeholders while positioning the Partnership as the premier regional economic development organization in the San Gabriel Valley.

**Key Responsibilities**

**Strategic Leadership**

* Lead the implementation and execution of the Partnership's Strategic Plan.
* Collaborate with the Board of Directors to establish organizational priorities, goals, and performance measures.
* Identify emerging economic, workforce, and public policy opportunities affecting the San Gabriel Valley.
* Position the Partnership as a recognized regional leader on issues impacting business competitiveness and economic growth.

**Organizational Management**

* Oversee the day\-to\-day operations of the organization and provide leadership to staff and consultants.
* Develop annual work plans, budgets, and organizational objectives.
* Ensure effective governance support for the Board of Directors and its committees.
* Foster a high\-performing, mission\-driven organizational culture.

**Revenue Development \& Membership Growth**

* Lead membership recruitment, retention, and engagement efforts.
* Develop and implement strategies to diversify and grow organizational revenue.
* Secure sponsorships, grants, partnerships, and other funding opportunities.
* Cultivate relationships with corporate, public\-sector, philanthropic, and community partners.

**Regional Advocacy \& External Relations**

* Serve as the chief spokesperson for the organization.
* Build and maintain strong relationships with elected officials, business executives, city managers, educational leaders, and community stakeholders.
* Represent the Partnership at regional, state, and national meetings and events.
* Advance public policy initiatives that support economic development, workforce development, infrastructure investment, housing, and business growth.

**Program \& Economic Development Leadership**

* Guide the development and implementation of programs and initiatives that support the Partnership's mission.
* Promote the San Gabriel Valley as a destination for investment, business attraction, and job creation.
* Strengthen collaboration among cities, educational institutions, employers, and workforce development partners.
* Oversee signature events, regional convenings, and economic development initiatives.

**Desired Qualifications**

The ideal candidate will possess:

* Demonstrated executive leadership experience in economic development, business organizations, chambers of commerce, public administration, nonprofit management, workforce development, or a related field.
* Proven success in organizational leadership, strategic planning, and revenue generation.
* Strong financial management and budgeting experience.
* Exceptional communication, public speaking, and relationship\-building skills.
* Experience working with boards of directors, elected officials, and diverse stakeholder groups.
* A track record of developing partnerships and leading collaborative initiatives.
* Knowledge of economic development, workforce development, public policy, and regional business issues.

**Education Requirements** 

A **bachelor's degree** from an accredited college or university is **required**. A master's degree in public administration, business administration, economic development, or a related field is preferred.

**Compensation \& Benefits**

The annual salary range for this position is **$135,000 to $170,000**, depending on qualifications and experience.

In addition, the Board of Directors may consider performance\-based compensation enhancements tied to the achievement of mutually agreed\-upon organizational revenue growth and fundraising targets. Any such incentives would be negotiated between the President \& CEO and the Executive Committee of the Board of Directors.

**The Partnership offers a competitive benefits package, including:**

* Employer\-paid medical insurance
* 401(k) retirement plan with a 3% employer match
* Four\-and\-one\-half\-day work week (half\-day Fridays)
* Three weeks paid vacation
* 80 hours of sick leave
* 48 hours of personal leave
* Ten paid holidays annually

**Application Process**

Interested candidates should submit:

* Cover Letter
* Resume
* Three Professional References\*

*\*References will only be contacted as part of a background check for a candidate that receives and accepts a conditional job offer.* 

Applications and questions should be submitted electronically to: **CEOJOB@sgvpartnership.org**

new_jobs.title: Executive Director
new_jobs.description:
**Executive Director**

**Position Description**

**Position Summary**

The Executive Director (ED) will provide leadership and direction to fulfill First Tee North Coast’s mission, vision and goals. Inspired by a genuine passion for empowering and helping youth succeed in life, ED will work diligently to promote and expand the efforts of the organization.

Reporting to and partnering with the FTNC Board of Directors, the ED will have overall strategic and operational responsibility for FTNC staff, programs, expansion and execution of the strategic plan.

The ED will be a highly visible member of the community, representing FTNC throughout the service area, including Marin, Sonoma, and Napa counties. The ED will embody FTNC’s strong commitment to its youth and will promote the organization throughout the community, accelerating its impact, resource development and brand recognition. The ED will be instrumental in helping FTNC achieve its full potential.

*Key Criteria for success of the role include*:

· Achieving strong fundraising success through a robust development and marketing communications program, including expanded engagement with individuals, corporations and foundations;

· Embodying a clear, inspiring, entrepreneurial vision and guiding the team to translate those strategies into effectively executed plans;

· Partnering effectively with the Board of Directors and staff to build on the success of the program to continually innovate and improve outcomes for youth;

· Ensuring program effectiveness through evaluation of quantitative and qualitative program analytics to articulate a comprehensive impact story;

· Expand and scale FTNC’s programming in additional high priority, at risk neighborhoods;

· Ability to collaborate effectively with the FTNC Board of Directors to develop strong board governance and fundraising practices.

**Responsibilities**

*Strategy:* ED will lead, partnering with the board, to develop strategies and goals to align and coordinate efforts to grow agency operations, programs, and resources. ED will create an annual development plan supporting the chapter strategic plan. ED will have fiscal responsibility of the organization along with responsibility to budget /forecast to align with the strategic plan.

*Fundraising:* ED, along with the Board and with the direction of the Fundraising Committee, identify, cultivate, solicit, and steward contributed revenues to support the organization while also activating key partners and volunteers to provide meaningful assistance. ED will establish revenue goals tied to strategic priorities and share quantitative progress assessments. Creative, donor\-centric stewardship is to be complemented by relationship and mission\-based acquisition of new donors of all levels, including major donors with the capacity to retain major gifts.

*Governance:* The ED and the board Governance Committee will work together, collaboratively, to determine how the organization can best achieve its mission. The ED will support each committee in their roles and responsibilities and work to facilitate a strong recruitment and development process.

*Marketing:* The ED will leverage national collateral with recently created brand insight work for FTNC to promote local approach to youth development through the game of golf. ED will be the local storyteller\-in\-residence and communicate the donor\-centric stories of impact and opportunities to empower kids by putting them on course for life.

*Management \& Compliance:* ED will provide energetic leadership, support, and recruitment assistance to the FTNC Board of Directors; delegate and oversee staff, and plan and oversee growth of office infrastructure. ED will ensure the FTNC chapter maintains compliance with the First Tee national organization.

**Qualifications**

· Nonprofit executive experience and philanthropic community engagement with a demonstrated history of success in coordinating and implementing fundraising strategies, grant/proposal writing, fundraising events, major gifts, campaigns, donor relationship building, and planned giving, preferably in the youth development field, and within the CA north bay communities including Marin, Sonoma, and Napa counties.

· Executive experience with nonprofit financials, budgeting, and fiscal management. Management of staff, contractors, and volunteers, including developing and collaborating with a nonprofit governing board, strategic partner alliances, and with the procurement of resources for direct\-service youth development programs.

· Passionate youth development ambassador with experience promoting access to and involvement in programs for young participants and their families, especially within underrepresented and underserved populations, and with volunteers, donors, and donor prospects and allied community partners.

· General knowledge of sports and its positive impact for youth development programming, particular with the game of golf, preferred.

· Confident strategic thinker, driven by conviction and knowledge, reliant on data analytics and context to inform decision\-making, with the fortitude to guide others in these practices.

· Excellent transparent and inclusive communicator who follows through and follows up. Ability to share thoughts and observations, and who consistently treats others equitably and with respect.

· Well\-organized and energetic; a lead\-by\-example doer, committed to personal excellence in leadership and execution who will uphold ethical standards and values of the nonprofit profession and FTNC.

· Knowledgeable professional, and also an avid and enthusiastic learner willing to ask questions and pursue professional development for self as well as staff and volunteers, fostering an active culture of philanthropic service and teamwork.

**To Apply**

Description:  



**Position Title:** Managing Director, Live Experiences

**Hours:** Full\-time (35\-hour workweek); Onsite presence required daily

**Role Classification:** Regular with Benefits

**Exemption:** Exempt

**Reports to:** Chief Experience Officer

**Compensation:** $120,000 \- $125,000

**ABOUT THE GARDENS OF GOLDEN GATE PARK**


The Gardens of Golden Gate Park unite three historic gardens—the landmark Conservatory of Flowers (1879\), the Japanese Tea Garden (1894\), and the 55\-acre San Francisco Botanical Garden (1940\)—featuring more than 10,000 different kinds of plants from around the world, from rare orchids and cloud forest species to California native plants and serene Japanese landscapes.


Our pioneering public\-private partnership between the San Francisco Recreation \& Parks Department and San Francisco Botanical Garden Society combines the city's expertise in horticulture and facilities with the nonprofit’s strengths in visitor experience, education, philanthropy, membership, marketing, curatorial services, interpretation, and community engagement. Together, we connect people to plants, the planet, and each other, cultivating an internationally renowned destination where conservation, natural beauty, and community flourish.


Learn about our Mission. Get oriented with our Strategic Plan.

**POSITION SUMMARY**


The Managing Director, Live Experiences is a senior leader responsible for translating the gardens’ institutional identity into elevated, memorable, and emotionally resonant experiences that strengthen the brand and drive business results. This is a general management role in the fullest sense: to lead the design, planning and execution of marquee experiences on a civic scale, reflecting the living heritage, operational agility, and “everybody’s gardens” ethic across 60\+ programs and 120\+ events delivered annually across three gardens.


What started as a bold idea in the botanical garden nearly 11 years ago has become a world\-renowned festival with Flower Piano. Building on our foundations of the gardens as star of the show, the launch of Lightscape last winter delivered one of the biggest light trails in North America and is our most significant program to date. The standard rises and our impact grows.


Today, the programs and events portfolio generate over $7M in annual revenue. From birding walks to cherry blossoms, to major institutional collaborations, our objective is clear: to position the Gardens of Golden Gate Park as San Francisco’s premier urban oasis serving over 1\.4 million people a year.


The Managing Director, Live Experiences is a critical leadership role within the Experience department, reporting to the Chief Experience Officer (CXO), who retains executive responsibility for strategic innovation and long\-range creative and financial vision for the portfolio. Your role is to own the delivery of that vision—at scale, at quality, and on margin.


As a key leader for the Gardens, the Managing Director, Live Experiences is expected to be present when it matters most, including frequent evenings, weekends, and holidays as required by events. Schedules are set in consultation with the CXO and adjusted to meet the needs of the operation.


The Managing Director supervises the Associate Director of Events and the Senior Program Manager and oversees a distributed team across three gardens, including contracted partners and operating personnel for mega ticketed events. Operations at this scale demand strategic leadership, financial discipline and profitability focus, decisive and timely execution, team alignment, operational excellence, and an unwavering commitment to visitor experience.


This role calls for someone who leads with ethics, transparency, and genuine commitment to our mission, and understands that live experience is both a service imperative and a revenue driver.


Requirements:
**RESPONSIBILITIES**

  

Strategic Leadership for Program \& Events Execution

* Develop and execute a comprehensive programs and events experience strategy aligned with the organization’s brand and business objectives, partnering closely with the CXO to ensure cohesive storytelling and program alignment across all three gardens.
* Own full\-cycle delivery of 60\+ programs and 120\+ events annually, from pre\-arrival communications through “return\-to\-life” post\-visit activities, including marquee, civic scale experiences such as Flower Piano, Lightscape, and future tentpole activations.
* Lead a team responsible for experience pipeline cultivation, experience design, modeling, placemaking, vendor management, and on\-site execution, with direct accountability for quality and timeline.


Partnerships \& Experience

* Steward partnership with the San Francisco Recreation \& Parks Department for the delivery of strategic programs including Lightscape, and collaborate with the Advancement team to identify, cultivate, and manage corporate sponsors and major institutional collaborations that expand the reach and relevance of the gardens.
* Design and deliver experiences with a solutions ethic, from large\-scale events serving broad, diverse populations to bespoke, high\-touch experiences for VIP audiences, grounded in a deep understanding of visitor expectations, local trends, and consumer behaviors.
* Activate the Conservatory of Flowers, Japanese Tea Garden, and San Francisco Botanical Garden through dedicated programming and after\-house experiences that attract new audiences, drive membership, and improve capacity utilization.


Brand \& Creative Standards

* Set and uphold visual and experiential quality standards for all programs and events across three garden sites.
* Promote plant\-focused programming that deepens connections between people, plants, and the planet.


Budget, Vendor \& Performance Management

new_jobs.title: Managing Director, Discovery and Insight
new_jobs.description:
**Company Description** **ABOUT TED**


TED is on a mission to discover and spread ideas that spark imagination, embrace possibility, and catalyze impact. Our organization is devoted to curiosity, reason, wonder, and the pursuit of knowledge — without an agenda. We welcome people from every discipline and culture who seek a deeper understanding of the world and connection with others, and we invite everyone to engage with ideas and activate them in your community.

**ABOUT THE AUDACIOUS PROJECT**


The Audacious Project is a collaborative funding platform that invites visionary changemakers to imagine their boldest ideas to solve the world’s most urgent challenges.


Housed at TED, the nonprofit dedicated to the belief that ideas change everything, Audacious works to surface ideas that are breathtakingly ambitious yet achievable and brings together a community of philanthropists to unlock the funding needed to make them real. Each year, Audacious selects a cohort of extraordinary leaders from around the world — visionaries with proximity to the problems they're solving, and bold plans to shift systems, lift up communities, and contribute to a healthier planet.


Since its launch in 2018, Audacious has helped to catalyze more than $8B for over 70 projects across a range of issue areas. Rooted in TED's belief that ideas can come from anywhere, Audacious focuses on the strength of the idea and the team behind it, not a fixed issue area. Funded projects span science, climate, technology, health, social safety net and justice.

 **Job Description** **Role Description**


The Managing Director, Discovery and Insight leads Audacious's global search for ambitious ideas and changemakers — finding, testing, and building the case for the ideas and leaders most worth backing each funding cycle. This role owns end\-to\-end curation, application, and diligence activities, ultimately leading to recommendations for the cohort of finalists selected annually.


The Managing Director's work is centered on: a strategy informed by continuous learning about systems\-level impact and Audacious' unique role within it; deep collaboration with applicants, donors, and partners nurtured over time; and leadership of a strong team to sustain the work.


Each year, the Discovery and Insight team conducts a global search to identify nonprofit ideas that are:

* Innovative and offer a unique approach to a significant problem with the potential to shift the status quo or the system they operate within
* Proximate \& Community\-Oriented in leadership and decision\-making structure (i.e., led by those who intimately understand the problem/opportunity, incorporating impacted communities in decision\-making, led by someone who has direct experience in/with the community served)
* Approaching an inflection point or needing a significant capital inflow to catalyze a differentiated level of impact that they haven’t received support for yet (rather than support for ongoing operations)
* Thoughtfully ambitious ideas that both inspire and convince — have an exciting, breakthrough vision (‘inspire’), clear outcomes, and evidence that the idea will have a significant and concrete impact (‘convince’)

 **Key Responsibilities**

**1\. Set and evolve the sourcing and curation strategy.**

* Advance sourcing and curation strategy for each cycle, forming a clear point of view on where Audacious should be learning, looking, collaborating, and why, ultimately carrying that strategy forward across cycles
* Develop a “future\-oriented” approach by monitoring and anticipating emerging trends and innovations that Audacious actively engages with or responds to
* Actively capture insights and lessons learned to refine and improve Audacious's sourcing and curation activities annually

 **2\. Lead selection and diligence.**

* Apply the sourcing and curation strategy to identify the ideas most worth Audacious's attention \- deciding what advances into the application stage, and bringing along multiple stakeholders and inputs along that decision\-making process
* Lead diligence on selected organizations, rigorously testing readiness, viability, and fit within Audacious's portfolio
* Build the recommendation — evidence, rationale, and risks included — that carries an idea into finalist selection

 **3\. Cultivate and steward the partners who bring ideas to Audacious.**

* Surface strong ideas by building genuine, ongoing relationships with social sector leaders, including funder and intermediary partners (incl. sourcing networks, collaboratives, and others)
* Steward key partner relationships by developing a deep understanding of each stakeholder’s goals, priorities, and aspirations
* Maintain stakeholder relationships continuously, within and across cycles, to ensure trust and rapport among all actors
* Represent Audacious's point of view in the field, using that standing to draw the most ambitious organizations and ideas toward the platform and to create richer opportunities for sector collaboration

 **4\. Build and nurture a team that explores the frontier of audacious social change.**

* Lead the Discovery and Insight vertical and its resourcing strategically, matching capacity to where the real work sits and bringing in the right technical expertise when needed
* Develop people who can source, diligence, and hold partner relationships credibly in their own right — building a team of strong practitioners so Audacious's discovery capacity grows beyond any single person
* Effectively steward relationships and actively collaborate with key Audacious partners (e.g., The Bridgespan Group) with whom we partner year\-round

 **Qualifications** **Career and Functional Experience**

**Essential**

* A leader who has set strategy in fast\-paced, entrepreneurial environments, and who can hold their own in rooms both with ultra\-high net worth individuals and issue experts and with their own team, making consequential decisions grounded in clear principles

minimum qualifications necessary to perform the essential functions of the position.
